sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Angel Oak Mortgage REIT 10-K: $143.7M Interest Income, $1.80 EPS
feed.summary: Angel Oak Mortgage REIT (AOMR) reported significant financial improvements in its latest 10-K filing, with interest income reaching $143.7 million and diluted earnings per common share at $1.80. The company's net income allocable to common stockholders grew to $44.0 million, supported by increased net interest income and a reduction in operating expenses. AOMR also highlighted substantial loan purchases and securitization activities, contributing to a positive market outlook for 2026.

feed.title: Prospective Costco gas station decision delayed by zoning board
feed.summary: A decision on a proposed Costco gas station in Plymouth, Massachusetts, has been delayed by the Zoning Board of Appeals due to resident opposition and concerns over water protection zones. This delay is part of a larger ongoing competition between Plymouth and Kingston to attract a new Costco and its associated tax revenue. The gas station's location within or near aquifer protection districts is a central point of contention, as both towns vie for the lucrative development while navigating environmental and regulatory challenges.

feed.title: Revival Gold Intercepts 4.2 g/t Gold Over 25 Meters at the Mercur Gold Project in Utah

The company expects to start its 2026 drilling program in April.

feed.title: Is Dow (DOW) Still Attractive After Recent Share Price Rebound?
feed.summary: This article evaluates whether Dow (DOW) remains an attractive investment after recent share price fluctuations. It uses a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis and Price-to-Sales (P/S) ratio, both of which suggest Dow is undervalued by 42.8% and screens as undervalued, respectively. The article also presents "bull" and "bear" case narratives for Dow, highlighting different future outlooks.

feed.title: How Strong Quarterly Results and Valuation Signals Will Impact Cardinal Health (CAH) Investors
feed.summary: Cardinal Health (CAH) recently reported quarterly revenue and earnings that surpassed analyst expectations, driven by strong demand and improved efficiency in its distribution operations. The company is positioned as a potential value-focused healthcare distributor with favorable valuation metrics and upgraded analyst assessments, despite ongoing risks related to regulatory and pricing pressures. The article suggests that while operational efficiency gains are positive, investors should remain aware of potential impacts from government regulation on its thin margins.

feed.title: Broadridge enhances its ProxyVote platform
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions has enhanced its ProxyVote platform to improve Pass-Through Voting capabilities for the asset management industry, making it easier for eligible investors to select their preferred voting policy. Vanguard is the first asset manager to adopt this new capability as part of its Investor Choice program, aiming to streamline participation for investors holding Vanguard funds on third-party brokerage platforms. This enhancement is designed to strengthen shareholder democracy and investor access by integrating Pass-Through Voting directly into the ProxyVote experience.

feed.title: Is First Solar’s (FSLR) Bigger Credit Line Quietly Redefining Its Capital Allocation Playbook?
feed.summary: First Solar (FSLR) recently announced strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results, with increased sales and net income, along with positive 2026 guidance. Importantly, the company secured a new US$1.50 billion unsecured revolving credit facility, significantly boosting its financial flexibility for manufacturing expansion and growth plans. This added liquidity is crucial for funding working capital and large U.S. projects, reinforcing the investment thesis around benefiting from domestic content incentives and a substantial contracted backlog, despite ongoing policy risks.

feed.title: PTC Inc. (NASDAQ:PTC) Meets Key Growth Criteria in "Little Book" Screen
feed.summary: PTC Inc. (NASDAQ:PTC) has been identified as meeting the key growth criteria outlined in Louis Navellier's "Little Book That Makes You Rich" investment strategy. The company demonstrates strong performance in analyst confidence with rising EPS estimates and consistent earnings beats. Furthermore, PTC shows accelerating growth in both sales and earnings, widening profit margins, robust free cash flow, and a high return on equity, making it an attractive candidate for growth-focused investors.

feed.title: Albertsons (ACI) director granted 10,734 RSUs and converts 3,081 units
feed.summary: Albertsons Companies director Wille Scott reported equity compensation activity, including the exercise of 3,081 time-based restricted stock units (RSUs) into Class A common shares after fully vesting on February 28, 2026. On the same date, Scott received a new grant of 10,734 time-based RSUs which will vest on February 27, 2027, provided continued service as a director. Following these transactions, Scott directly holds 21,701 shares of Class A common stock and 10,734 restricted stock units.

feed.title: Amex Platinum vs. Chase Sapphire Reserve: The Winner Might Surprise You in March 2026
feed.summary: This article compares the American Express Platinum Card and the Chase Sapphire Reserve, two premium travel credit cards, to determine which offers better value for most travelers in March 2026. Despite the Chase Sapphire Reserve's recent fee increase and robust travel credits, the author argues that the Amex Platinum Card ultimately provides superior VIP treatment, broader airport lounge access, and a more compelling welcome bonus for those willing to utilize its extensive credit package. The article details the benefits, fees, and earning structures of both cards, concluding that the Amex Platinum Card is generally the better choice, especially for those new to premium travel cards.

feed.title: S&P 500 Futures Steady in Premarket Trading; Moderna, SSR Mining Lead

feed.title: Big investors have been fleeing for-sale housing market, even before Trump ordered ban
feed.summary: Large institutional investors are increasingly selling single-family rental homes, with data showing them to be net sellers across major markets like Dallas, Philadelphia, and Houston. This trend began even before President Trump's executive order aimed at restricting institutional home purchases, as investors are finding better risk-adjusted returns by divesting and pivoting towards build-to-rent properties. The market shift reflects a return to capital recycling after post-2020 home price surges and elevated borrowing costs, with companies like Invitation Homes and AMH focusing on new construction and build-to-rent communities.

feed.title: Antitrust trial begins for Live Nation and Ticketmaster over market dominance
feed.summary: A high-stakes antitrust trial has begun against Live Nation and Ticketmaster, with the U.S. Justice Department seeking to break up what it characterizes as an illegal monopoly in the live entertainment industry. The lawsuit, supported by states like Indiana, argues that the company's dominance stifles competition, harms artists, and overcharges consumers, citing the 2022 Taylor Swift ticket debacle as an example. Live Nation, however, denies having monopoly power and asserts its role in supporting musical artists.
